title: tordylium cordatum
intro: Tordylium cordatum, also known as Wild Fennel or Pepperwort, is a flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the family Brassicaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Tordylium cordatum include its ability to relieve respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
